name: Border with Portugal - from Aldeia da Ponte to Fuenteguinaldo — circular through the lands of Rebollar
name_original: Frontera con Portugal - de Aldeia da Ponte a Fuenteguinaldo — circular por tierras del Rebollar
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Casillas De Flores, a rural village in Salamanca, Spain, traverse diverse landscapes characterized by natural features such as the oak forests of El Rebollar and the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Gata. The region offers varied terrain, from river valleys along the Tormes River to higher elevations in the Sierra de Béjar and near Pico Jálama. Cyclists can expect a mix of paved surfaces and well-maintained tracks, providing a range of experiences. Best road cycling routes around Casillas De Flores The most popular road cycling route is Sierra de Gata Loop via Puerto Viejo and San Martín, a 26.4 miles (42.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route features ascents through the Sierra de Gata. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Border with Portugal - from Aldeia da Ponte to Fuenteguinaldo — circular through the lands of Rebollar, a moderate 39.3 miles (63.3 km) path. This route explores the border region with Portugal and the oak woodlands of El Rebollar. Local road cyclists also love the Fuenteguinaldo – Navasfrías Town Center loop from Fuenteguinaldo, a 52.5 miles (84.4 km) trail leading through the rural landscapes between Fuenteguinaldo and Navasfrías, often completed in about 4 hours 9 minutes. Road cycling around Casillas De Flores is defined by oak forests, river valleys, and mountainous areas like the Sierra de Gata. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Casillas De Flores's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Casillas de Flores?
A: There are 17 road cycling routes around Casillas de Flores listed on komoot, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate rides to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Casillas de Flores?
A: The region around Casillas de Flores offers a varied terrain for road cycling. You'll encounter oak forests, such as those in El Rebollar, river valleys along the Tormes River, and mountainous areas like the Sierra de Gata and Sierra de Béjar. Routes feature a mix of paved surfaces and well-maintained tracks, providing a diverse cycling experience.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, Casillas de Flores offers several challenging road cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1275619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuenteguinaldo – Navasfrías Town Center loop from Fuenteguinaldo&lt;/a&gt; covers 84.4 km with over 1260 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21951041" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín de Trevejo – Navasfrías Town Center loop from Casillas de Flores&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 1400 meters of ascent.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural features I can see along the routes?
A: Road cycling routes in this area often pass through beautiful natural landscapes. You can experience the oak woodlands of El Rebollar, enjoy views of the Sierra de Gata, and explore the border region with Portugal. While cycling, you might also encounter natural landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4958182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Jálama Summit&lt;/a&gt; in the Sierra de Gata, offering spectacular views, or the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5440256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aldeia da Ponte Stream&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Casillas de Flores are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025826316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Gata Loop via Puerto Viejo and San Martín&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025861383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Border with Portugal - from Aldeia da Ponte to Fuenteguinaldo — circular through the lands of Rebollar&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the oak woodlands of El Rebollar.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Casillas de Flores?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be warm, especially at midday, while winters might bring colder conditions, particularly in higher elevations like the Sierra de Béjar.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes near Casillas de Flores?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation, some sections or shorter loops might be suitable for families looking for less strenuous rides. The region offers varied terrain, so it's best to check the elevation profile and distance of specific routes to ensure they match your family's ability. Look for routes with lower elevation gain and shorter distances.

Q: What kind of accommodation is available for cyclists in Casillas de Flores and nearby?
A: Casillas de Flores is a rural village, so accommodation options typically include charming rural guesthouses, casas rurales, and small hotels. Many cater to outdoor enthusiasts, and some may offer facilities like secure bike storage. It's advisable to check with individual establishments regarding cyclist-specific amenities.

Q: Where can I find cafes or restaurants along the road cycling routes?
A: As you cycle through the villages and towns in the region, you'll find local cafes and restaurants. Larger towns like Fuenteguinaldo or Navasfrías, which are often part of longer routes, will have more options for refreshments and meals. It's a good idea to plan your stops in advance, especially on more remote sections.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in Casillas de Flores?
A: As a rural village, Casillas de Flores typically offers accessible parking, often in public spaces within the village or near main access points. For specific routes, it's usually possible to find parking in the starting village or designated areas. Always ensure you park responsibly and respect local regulations.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Casillas de Flores?
A: Road cyclists frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from the serene oak forests to the challenging mountain passes, and the quiet roads that allow for an immersive experience. The opportunity to explore both Spanish and Portuguese border regions, combined with the natural beauty of areas like the Sierra de Gata, makes it a popular destination.

Q: Are there any less crowded road cycling routes in the area?
A: Given its rural setting, many routes around Casillas de Flores offer a tranquil cycling experience with less traffic compared to more urban areas. Routes that venture deeper into natural parks or along less-traveled regional roads, such as those exploring the oak woodlands of El Rebollar, are likely to be less crowded.
tips: Wetland where a pond full of water appears with a beautiful bridge that crosses it. Nearby, there is the Chapel of Santo António, close to the road and small at the same time!
To the southwest of the province of Salamanca is the town of Fuenteguinaldo, bordering the province of Cáceres and the kingdom of Portugal. This municipality belongs to the region of Ciudad Rodrigo
